content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"StatefulSet","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"mongodb-replica","namespace":"mongodb"},"spec":{"replicas":3,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"mongodb-replica"}},"serviceName":"mongodb-replica-svc","template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"mongodb-replica"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["mongod","--replSet","mongodb-replica","--bind_ip_all"],"image":"mongo:6.0.5","name":"mongod","ports":[{"containerPort":27017,"name":"mongodb","protocol":"TCP"}],"resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"1","memory":"1Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"0.5","memory":"512Mi"}},"volumeMounts":[{"mountPath":"/data/db","name":"data-volume"}]}]}},"volumeClaimTemplates":[{"metadata":{"name":"data-volume"},"spec":{"accessModes":["ReadWriteOnce"],"resources":{"requests":{"storage":"10Gi"}}}}]}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-24T07:19:33Z"
  generation: 1
  name: mongodb-replica
  namespace: mongodb
  resourceVersion: "995353"
  uid: 909c357b-6ddf-4ab4-957a-f964528de6ef
spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady
  replicas: 3
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mongodb-replica
  serviceName: mongodb-replica-svc
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: mongodb-replica
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - mongod
        - --replSet
        - mongodb-replica
        - --bind_ip_all
        image: mongo:6.0.5
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: mongod
        ports:
        - containerPort: 27017
          name: mongodb
          protocol: TCP
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 1Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 512Mi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /data/db
          name: data-volume
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
  updateStrategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
    type: RollingUpdate
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - apiVersion: v1
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      name: data-volume
    spec:
      accessModes:
      - ReadWriteOnce
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
      volumeMode: Filesystem
    status:
      phase: Pending
status:

  collisionCount: 0

  observedGeneration: 1

  updateRevision: mongodb-replica-6c88fc8dc4
  updatedReplicas: 3

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture:
- 3-member MongoDB replica set running in the `mongodb` namespace
- StatefulSet `mongodb-replica` with 3 replicas
- Headless service `mongodb-replica-svc`
- Each pod has its own PVC `data-volume-mongodb-replica-N`
- The MongoDB replica set is named `mongodb-replica`
- Pods use DNS: `mongodb-replica-N.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017`
- No authentication (no keyfile or auth mentioned in the mongod command)
- There are secrets for `admin-user-password` and `mongo-keyfile`, but the StatefulSet doesn't actually enable authentication in the mongod command

To scale to 5 members, I'll scale the StatefulSet from 3 to 5 replicas, which will automatically create pods mongodb-replica-3 and mongodb-replica-4 with their corresponding PVCs, then add the new members to the replica set using `rs.add()`. This is safe because scaling the StatefulSet up simply adds new pods without affecting existing ones.

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. The StatefulSet has 3 replicas with a headless service and volumeClaimTemplates. I'll scale to 5 by:
1. Scaling the StatefulSet (automatically provisions new PVCs)
2. Adding the 2 new members to the replica set config via `rs.add()`

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval "rs.status().members.map(m => ({id: m._id, name: m.name, state: m.stateStr}))" 2>&1
input.description: Check current replica set member status

content.thinking: Good. Now I'll scale the StatefulSet to 5 replicas.

content.text: Replica set is healthy (1 PRIMARY + 2 SECONDARY). Now scaling the StatefulSet to 5 — new pods and PVCs will be provisioned automatically.

content.text: Both new pods are up. Now I'll add them to the MongoDB replica set as new secondaries.

content.text: Both `rs.add()` calls returned `ok: 1`. Now wait for the new members to finish initial sync and reach SECONDARY state:

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 12); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval "
    rs.status().members.map(m => m.stateStr).join(',')
  " 2>&1)
  echo "[$i] $STATUS"
  echo "$STATUS" | grep -qv "STARTUP" && echo "$STATUS" | grep -qv "RECOVERING" && break
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Wait for new members to reach SECONDARY state

description: Wait for new members to reach SECONDARY state
